common.heroFooter: Free assessment • No obligation • Your information is private

home.heroTitle: Injured in an Accident? See What Your Case May Be Worth in 60 Seconds
home.heroSubtitle: Answer a few questions about your accident and see:
home.heroItem1: If you may have a case
home.heroItem2: What your case may be worth
home.heroItem3: How long it could take to settle
home.trustBar: Helping California injury victims understand their options
home.trust1: Thousands of injury case patterns analyzed
home.trust2: Optional matching with vetted personal injury attorneys
home.trust4: Plain-English case value and next-step guidance
home.howItWorksIntro: Start with the facts you know now. ClearCaseIQ turns your answers into a plain-English case snapshot and next-step options.
home.step1Desc: Answer guided questions about the accident, injuries, treatment, and insurance.
home.step2Desc: See case strength, possible value range, timeline, and missing evidence.
home.step3Desc: For some straightforward claims, you may be able to prepare and send a settlement demand yourself. You can also request attorney review before signing any release.
home.commonCaseTypes: Injury Cases We Can Help Screen
home.commonCaseTypesDesc: Start with any accident type. If your case needs a lawyer, we can help route it to the right kind of attorney.
home.reportIncludes: What You Get After the Free Assessment
home.reportIncludesDesc: A practical case snapshot you can use before deciding whether to handle next steps yourself or request attorney review.
home.reportItem5: Self-help demand package for straightforward claims
home.whyUse: Why Start Here Before Calling a Lawyer?
home.whyUseDesc: Get clarity, control, and next steps before you share your case broadly.
home.whyUseItem1: Know whether your case may be worth pursuing before you spend days calling firms.
home.whyUseItem2: Understand the value drivers insurers and attorneys usually look at.
home.whyUseItem3: See what documents could strengthen your claim.
home.whyUseItem4: Choose the path that fits: self-help demand package, attorney review, or save your report for later.
home.step3DescAlt: For some straightforward claims, you may be able to prepare and send a settlement demand yourself. For serious injuries, disputed liability, liens, or any release, attorney review is strongly recommended.
home.testimonial1Quote: I finally understood what my case could be worth before I ever spoke to a lawyer. The process felt respectful and clear.
home.testimonial2Quote: Clear steps, plain language, and I knew my data was private. That mattered after the accident.
home.testimonial3Quote: Within a day I was connected to a firm that actually handles injury cases like mine—not a random call center.
home.finalCtaHelper: Get your case summary, estimate, and next-step checklist.
home.heroReassurance: Takes about 60 seconds · No signup required · 100% confidential
home.securityBadge: Encrypted intake · HIPAA-aware handling of medical details
home.ratingLabel: Rated 5 out of 5 by example users
home.legalDisclaimer: ClearCaseIQ is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice, and no attorney-client relationship is created by using this site. Case estimates are informational only and are not a guarantee of any outcome. Attorney review is optional and occurs only with your consent.
home.faqQ1: Is the case assessment really free?
home.faqA1: Yes. The case assessment is free, requires no signup to start, and there is no obligation to hire an attorney.
home.faqQ2: Will my information be shared with attorneys?
home.faqA2: Only with your consent. Your intake is private and encrypted, and we share your details with an attorney only if you choose to request attorney review or a match.
home.faqQ3: How accurate is the estimated case value?
home.faqA3: The estimate is an informational range based on patterns in similar injury cases and the facts you provide. It is not legal advice or a guarantee of any specific result.
home.faqA4: Most people complete the guided questions in about 60 seconds and receive a case snapshot immediately.

footer.tagline: Free injury case assessments and attorney matching.
footer.privacyLine: Private intake. No obligation. Attorney sharing only with your consent.
footer.trustRow: Private • Secure • No Obligation • Attorney Sharing Only With Consent
footer.disclaimer: ClearCaseIQ is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice. Use of this platform does not create an attorney-client relationship. Attorney review is optional and only occurs with user consent.

intake.timePromise: Free case assessment • About 60 seconds • No obligation
intake.startHeadline: Find out if you may have a personal injury case in 60 seconds
intake.startHelper: Answer a few guided questions. You will get a case snapshot, evidence checklist, and next-step options.
intake.skipReassurance: Most people finish in about 60 seconds. You can skip anything you do not know.
intake.answerReassurance: Answer what you know. You can skip uncertain details now and update your case after the report is created.
intake.privacyNote: Private intake. We only share your case with attorneys if you choose attorney review.
intake.tapToContinue: Tap any option to continue automatically
intake.injuryType: What type of incident caused your injury?
intake.injuryTypeHelp: This helps us route the right questions and match you with attorneys.
intake.whenHelp: This helps us check statute of limitations and timeline.
intake.whereHelp: This helps us match you with attorneys in your area.
intake.narrativeHelp: This helps us understand your case and assess liability.
intake.injurySeverity: How serious are your injuries so far?
intake.medicalTreatment: Have you received medical care for this injury?
intake.selectAllApply: Select all that apply, then click Next.
intake.evidenceHelp: Upload one file or select multiple files at once. Evidence can strengthen your case and improve your estimate.
intake.casePostureHelp: This helps us route your case appropriately.
intake.narrativePlaceholder: Describe the incident in your own words...
intake.optionalUpload: Optional: You can upload photos or documents later to strengthen your case.
intake.tellMore: Tell us more about your injury
intake.vehicle_photosVideo: Do you have photos or video?
intake.vehicle_propertyDamage: How severe was the vehicle damage?
intake.vehicle_defendantQuestion: Who was driving the other vehicle?
intake.slip_employeesKnew: Did employees know about the hazard?
intake.slip_hazardDuration: Had the hazard been there for a while?
intake.medmal_additionalTreatment: Did the mistake require additional treatment?
intake.medmal_knowProvider: Do you know the doctor or hospital name?
intake.dog_ownershipQuestion: Was the dog owned by someone?
intake.dog_priorAggressionQuestion: Did the dog have a history of aggression?
intake.product_injuryCauseQuestion: How did the product cause injury?
intake.toxic_doctorQuestion: Did a doctor link your illness to the exposure?
intake.case_posture_insurance_help: This helps us estimate timelines and any lien or reimbursement issues (for example, Medicare).
intake.insurance_healthCoverageQuestion: Do you have health coverage that has paid — or may pay — for treatment related to this injury? (Private insurance, Medicare, Medicaid, workers’ comp, etc.)
intake.insurance_typesRequired: Select at least one option, or go back and choose “Not sure” above.
intake.insurance_medicarePlanHelp: Medicare can affect reimbursement and notices your attorney may need to handle.
intake.insurance_medicare_original: Original Medicare (Part A and/or Part B)
intake.insurance_coverageRequired: Please tell us if you have health coverage related to this injury.
intake.insurance_medicarePlanRequired: Please select the Medicare type that best applies.
intake.casePosture_spokenToInsurance: Have you spoken with the insurance company?
intake.casePosture_hiredLawyer: Have you hired a lawyer already?
intake.casePosture_receivedOffer: Have you received a settlement offer?
intake.casePosture_wantLawyer: Would you like to speak with a lawyer?
intake.consent_intro: You're about to see your personalized ClearCaseIQ Case Intelligence Report.
intake.consent_tos: I agree to the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy
intake.consent_ml: I consent to AI processing of my information for case analysis
intake.skipForNow: Skip for now — you can upload later
intake.almostDoneContinue: You're almost done. Click Next to continue.
intake.almostDoneEvidence: You're almost done. Click Next to continue to evidence upload.
intake.caseDetails_progressTitle: These answers strengthen your Liability Score
intake.review_whatHappenedEmpty: You can add a short description now or update it later.
intake.review_whatHappenedHelper: Plain-language accident details help attorneys understand fault faster.
intake.review_whereWhenHelper: This supports venue and deadline checks.
intake.review_injuriesHelper: It is okay if this is only an early estimate.
intake.review_treatmentHelper: Medical treatment details are one of the strongest value signals.
intake.review_valuationHelper: Prior injuries, body parts, procedures, surgery, and future care can change settlement and trial values.
intake.review_insuranceHelper: Coverage, liens, income impact, and policy limits can improve estimate accuracy.
intake.review_legalHelper: Fault, insurance contact, representation, and settlement history shape attorney interest.
intake.review_documentsHelper: No documents yet is okay. You can still get a preliminary assessment.
intake.insight_imaging: Diagnostic imaging may improve estimate confidence.
intake.insight_injections: Injections or procedures can affect settlement posture.
intake.insight_surgery: Surgery being discussed may increase trial exposure.
intake.insight_missedWork: Missed work may increase economic damages.
intake.insight_noPrior: No prior injuries reported for the same areas.
intake.insight_prior: Prior injuries may affect causation and settlement compression.
intake.insight_offerAnchor: A prior offer gives the model a useful settlement anchor.
intake.insight_fault: Clearer fault facts can improve settlement leverage.
intake.insight_uploads: Uploaded documents improve confidence in the assessment.
intake.insight_fallback1: Your report will highlight missing details that can improve the estimate later.
intake.insight_fallback2: Treatment, documents, and fault details can refine settlement confidence.
intake.when_helper: This helps us check statute of limitations and timeline.
intake.narrative_heading: Tell the story in your own words.
intake.narrative_helper: A few sentences is enough. Skip anytime—you can edit later.
intake.narrative_placeholder: Example: Rear-ended at a red light; police report; urgent care next day.
intake.narrative_tip: Tip: what happened, fault, witnesses, photos, or report.
intake.narrative_skip: I am not sure. I will add this later.
intake.contact_heading: Where can we send your case report?
intake.contact_helper: Optional. Adding an email or phone lets us send your report and follow up if you do not finish. We never share your contact info without your permission.
intake.contact_emailError: Enter a valid email address, or leave it blank.
intake.treatment_heading: What treatment have you had so far?
intake.treatment_helper: Select everything that applies. It is okay if treatment is still being scheduled.
intake.treatment_tip: Medical records and bills usually improve estimate confidence more than any other document type.
intake.treatment_skip: I have not had treatment yet
intake.injuryDetails_helper: These answers improve the accuracy of your case value estimate.
intake.injuryDetails_confidenceTip: Upload imaging or treatment records to improve accuracy.
intake.injuryDetails_whereInjuredHelper: Select all body regions that hurt or were diagnosed.
intake.injuryDetails_testingHelper: Select any testing or treatment that has happened or is scheduled.
intake.injuryDetails_diagnosesQuestion: Have doctors mentioned any of these diagnoses?
intake.injuryDetails_diagnosesHelper: Select known findings like fractures, TBI, concussion, or herniation.
intake.injuryDetails_lifeImpact: How has this affected your life?
intake.injuryDetails_lifeImpactHelper: These details help estimate pain and daily disruption. Work and income impact comes later.
intake.injuryDetails_headSymptoms: Did you experience any of these?
intake.injuryDetails_injectionsMore: Tell us more about the injections.
intake.injuryDetails_futureTreatmentQuestion: Has a doctor recommended future treatment?
intake.vehicle_evidenceHelper: Select everything you have or know about. Police reports, tickets, witnesses, and photos can help show who was at fault.
intake.vehicle_photos: Photos of vehicles, scene, or injuries
intake.vehicle_redLight: The other driver ran a red light or stop sign
intake.vehicle_dui: DUI, alcohol, or drug use by the other driver
intake.slip_awarenessHelper: Select everything that applies. These details help show whether the property owner may have known about the hazard or had time to fix it.
intake.evidence_helper: Upload documents now or after receiving your report.
intake.evidence_sectionEvidenceHelper: Photos, videos, and official reports from the incident.
intake.evidence_sectionMedicalHelper: Bills and records that show treatment, diagnosis, and costs.
intake.evidence_sectionInsuranceHelper: Letters, denials, offers, and adjuster communications.
intake.evidence_sectionIncomeHelper: Documents that show missed work or reduced income.
intake.evidence_photosHelper: Helps document injuries, vehicle damage, and scene conditions.
intake.evidence_videosHelper: Helps show impact, fault, traffic signals, or unsafe conditions.
intake.evidence_policeReportHelper: Helps determine liability and case strength.
intake.evidence_medicalRecordsHelper: Helps assess injury severity and treatment needs.
intake.evidence_insuranceLettersHelper: Helps identify denials, low offers, and coverage issues.
intake.evidence_wageVerificationHelper: Helps estimate lost income and economic damages.
intake.financial_heading: How has this injury affected you financially?
intake.financial_helper: These answers help estimate the economic value of your case.
intake.financial_completeNote: You have completed the core information attorneys use to evaluate case value.
intake.financial_medicalCostsHelper: Medical bills and future care often drive case value.
intake.financial_exactAmountLabel: Approximate total, if you know it
intake.financial_exactAmountHelper: Optional. A specific amount values large cases more accurately than the "$50k+" range.
intake.financial_billsCompleteQuestion: Do the bills you're providing cover all your treatment so far?
intake.financial_billsCompleteHelper: If yes, we treat your documented bills as the total. If no, your estimate is kept as a floor and we'll prompt for the rest.
intake.financial_healthInsuranceQuestion: Did you have health insurance when you received treatment?
intake.financial_insurancePaidQuestion: Did health insurance pay any medical bills?
intake.financial_whoPaidHelper: This is very important to attorneys.
intake.financial_workImpactHelper: Lost income can increase economic damages.
intake.legal_heading: A few final questions about your claim
intake.legal_helper: Insurance and legal status help us understand how attorneys may evaluate your claim.
intake.legal_vehicleInsuranceHelper: Can help identify additional sources of recovery. Most people are not sure about these details.
intake.legal_autoInsuranceQuestion: Did you have auto insurance at the time?
intake.legal_umUimHelper: Uninsured/Underinsured Motorist coverage may provide additional compensation if the other driver has little or no insurance.
intake.legal_otherPartyQuestion: Do you know anything about the other party's insurance?
intake.legal_caseReviewHelper: Help us understand how attorneys may evaluate your claim.
intake.legal_partialFaultQuestion: Could someone claim you were partially at fault?
intake.legal_reportedQuestion: Have you reported the accident to insurance?
intake.legal_attorneyQuestion: Do you currently have an attorney?
intake.legal_acceptedQuestion: Have you accepted a settlement offer?
intake.legal_settledWarning: Settled cases may not qualify for attorney review.
intake.review_helper: Quick check before we create the report.
intake.review_confidenceHelper: Based on treatment detail, documents, fault context, and missing information.
intake.review_ready: Ready to generate your ClearCaseIQ assessment. Missing details will be shown as next steps in your report.
intake.consent_approxDate: Based on an approximate incident date. An attorney will confirm the exact deadline.
intake.consent_confirmHelper: Please confirm these permissions so we can display your case analysis.
intake.consent_agreeTerms: I agree to the Terms & Privacy Policy
intake.consent_agreeAi: I consent to AI-assisted case analysis
intake.consent_privacySecure: Your information remains private and secure.
intake.uploadFailed_one: Your report was created, but {count} document failed to attach
intake.uploadFailed_many: Your report was created, but {count} documents failed to attach
intake.draft_savedProgress: We saved your progress, so you can pick up where you left off.
intake.savedAnswer_hint: Tap another option to update your answer.
intake.sol_noDeadline: We could not confirm the deadline from the current facts.
intake.sol_selectState: Select the state and county to check your filing deadline early.
intake.sol_dayRemaining: About {days} day remaining based on your current answers.
intake.sol_daysRemaining: About {days} days remaining based on your current answers.
intake.sol_approxNote: This estimate uses an approximate incident date.
intake.stepTitles_financial_impact: How has this injury affected you financially?
intake.error_submitFailed: Failed to submit. Please try again.
intake.legal_insurerContactQuestion: Has the insurance company contacted you or made an offer?
intake.legal_offerAmountQuestion: About how much was the offer?
intake.legal_coverageLimitsQuestion: Do you know the other party's insurance limits?
